title: Wren's Big Helper Day
synopsis: When Wren's little brother Max feels left out of helping with family chores, Wren discovers that being a good big sister means finding special ways for everyone to contribute, no matter how small they are.

pages.text: Saturday was Helper Day at Wren's house. Mom made pancakes shaped like smiley faces. "Today we clean and organize together," said Mom. "Everyone gets a special job." Wren bounced in her chair. She loved Helper Day! Her little brother Max clapped his hands. "Me help! Me help!" he said.

illustration.description: A bright kitchen scene with a Black mother with natural hair making smiley face pancakes at the stove. Wren, a biracial girl with curly brown hair and big brown eyes, sits at a colorful breakfast table looking excited. Little Max, a toddler with darker skin and tight curls, claps his hands in a high chair. Warm yellow morning light streams through windows.
illustration.alt: Mom making smiley pancakes while Wren and Max sit excitedly at the breakfast table

pages.text: Dad came downstairs with his hair sticking up funny. "Good morning, my helper crew!" he said with a big yawn. Dad had brown skin like Max and kind eyes behind his glasses. "I need help organizing the garage today." Wren jumped up. "I can carry boxes!" Max stood on his chair. "Max carry too!"

illustration.description: Dad, a Black man with glasses and messy morning hair, stretches his arms with a sleepy smile. Wren stands proudly next to the table with her hands on her hips, looking determined. Max tries to stand on his chair, wobbling slightly. The kitchen has warm wood cabinets and cheerful family photos on the walls.
illustration.alt: Dad arriving for breakfast while Wren and Max volunteer to help with chores

pages.text: After breakfast, Mom gave out the jobs. "Wren, you help Dad with boxes. I'll clean the kitchen." She looked at Max. "And you can... help me wash dishes." But Max wanted to carry boxes like Wren. "No dishes!" Max said. "Big boxes!" Wren felt proud that she got the important job.

illustration.description: Mom points to a colorful chore chart on the refrigerator. Wren looks proud and excited, standing tall with her chest puffed out. Max looks disappointed, his arms crossed and bottom lip pushed out in a pout. The kitchen background shows breakfast dishes and the family's cheerful home decorations.
illustration.alt: Mom assigning chores while Wren looks proud and Max looks disappointed

pages.text: In the garage, Wren and Dad moved boxes from high shelves. "You're such a good helper, Wren," said Dad. Wren smiled big. She could reach tall places and carry heavy things. Max watched from the doorway. His eyes looked sad. "I help too," he said quietly.

illustration.description: A cluttered garage with Dad and Wren working together, moving colorful storage boxes. Dad smiles proudly at Wren as she reaches for a box. In the background, little Max stands in the doorway looking small and sad, his shoulders drooping. The garage has bikes, tools, and family equipment stored around.
illustration.alt: Dad and Wren working in the garage while Max watches sadly from the doorway

pages.text: Dad tried to find something for Max. "Here, buddy. You can hold this small box." But the box was empty and light. Max knew it wasn't a real job. He sat on the ground with the empty box. "This not heavy," Max said. "Not important." Wren kept working with Dad on the real boxes.

illustration.description: Max sits on the concrete garage floor holding a tiny, obviously empty box, looking dejected. His expression shows he knows this isn't a real job. Behind him, Dad and Wren continue moving substantial boxes, creating a contrast between the meaningful work and Max's token task. The lighting emphasizes Max's isolation in the foreground.
illustration.alt: Max sitting sadly with an empty box while Dad and Wren do real work behind him

pages.text: Max tried to help anyway. He picked up Dad's screwdriver. It was too heavy and clattered to the floor. "Careful!" said Dad. Max tried to move a medium box. It tipped over and things spilled out. "Oh no!" said Dad. "Max, maybe you should go inside." Max's eyes filled with tears. Wren felt her tummy feel funny watching Max.

illustration.description: Max looks upset as items scatter around him on the garage floor from a tipped box. Dad has a concerned but gentle expression, reaching toward the mess. Wren watches with a worried, guilty expression, her eyebrows furrowed. The scene shows the chaos of scattered toys and supplies with warm but dramatic lighting capturing the emotional moment.
illustration.alt: Max crying after making a mess while Dad looks concerned and Wren feels worried

pages.text: Inside, Max sat on the couch looking very sad. Mom was washing dishes and didn't see him crying. Wren found Max with tears on his cheeks. "What's wrong?" Wren asked. "I too little," said Max. "No good helper." Wren's heart felt squishy. She didn't like seeing Max cry.

illustration.description: Max curls up sadly on a plush sofa, wiping tears from his cheeks with small fists. Wren kneels beside the couch looking concerned and caring, her head tilted with empathy. The living room has warm, cozy colors with family pictures and soft lighting. Mom can be seen in the background at the kitchen sink.
illustration.alt: Wren comforting crying Max on the living room couch

pages.text: Wren thought hard. Max was little, but he was still part of the family. "Wait here," Wren told Max. She went to find Dad. "Dad, I have an idea. Max wants to help for real." Dad stopped organizing. "What's your idea, sweetie?" Wren whispered her plan in Dad's ear. Dad smiled. "That's a wonderful idea, Wren."

illustration.description: Wren stands on her tiptoes to whisper in Dad's ear in the garage. Dad leans down with a warm, impressed smile spreading across his face. His eyes crinkle with pride behind his glasses. The garage setting has afternoon light filtering in, and both characters have animated, expressive faces showing the sweet moment of connection.
illustration.alt: Wren whispering her helpful idea to Dad in the garage

pages.text: Wren and Dad went back to Max. "Max," said Dad, "we need your help with something very important." Max looked up with hopeful eyes. "We need someone small to crawl under the workbench," said Wren. "Someone who fits in tiny spaces." Max sat up straight. "I small! I fit!" he said, wiping his nose.

illustration.description: Max's expression transforms from sadness to excitement and hope. His eyes grow wide and bright as he points to himself proudly. Wren and Dad crouch beside him with encouraging smiles. The lighting captures this turning point moment with warm, hopeful tones. Max's posture changes from slumped to eager and upright.
illustration.alt: Max looking excited and hopeful as Wren and Dad ask for his special help

pages.text: Under the workbench were lost balls and toy cars. Only Max could fit in the small space. "I see a red ball!" Max called out. He wiggled under and pushed it to Wren. "And a blue car!" Dad cheered. "Great job, Max!" Max giggled and kept searching. "More toys!" he said happily.

illustration.description: Max crawls happily under a wooden workbench, his little legs sticking out as he reaches for colorful toys. Wren and Dad kneel nearby, collecting the rescued toys with big smiles. The scene is filled with bright primary colors from the recovered balls and toy cars scattered around. Max's muffled giggles seem to fill the space.
illustration.alt: Max crawling under the workbench to retrieve lost toys while Wren and Dad cheer him on

pages.text: Max found seven balls, three toy cars, and Mom's missing earring! "You're the best small-space helper ever!" said Wren. Max beamed with pride. "I good helper!" he said. Dad picked Max up and spun him around. "The best!" said Dad. Mom came out and saw her earring. "Max! You found it!" she said with a big hug.

illustration.description: Dad lifts a giggling Max high in the air, spinning him around with joy. Wren claps excitedly beside them. Mom runs over with her arms outstretched, her face showing delighted surprise. Around them are piles of the rescued toys and the found earring sparkling in the light. Everyone's faces radiate happiness and celebration.
illustration.alt: Dad spinning Max in celebration while Wren claps and Mom approaches with joy

pages.text: The family finished Helper Day together. Max helped sort the found toys by color. Wren helped him make neat piles. "Red here, blue there," said Max, feeling very important. "You're a great teacher, Wren," said Mom. "And Max, you're our special helper." Max grinned. "I love Helper Day!" he said. Wren hugged her little brother tight. "Me too," she said.

illustration.description: The whole family sits together on the clean garage floor, sorting colorful toys into organized piles. Max concentrates seriously on his sorting task while Wren guides him gently. Mom and Dad watch proudly in the background. The late afternoon sun creates a warm, golden glow over the scene. Everyone looks content and connected as a family.
illustration.alt: The family working together happily to sort toys, with Max feeling proud and important

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: Why was Max feeling sad?
choices: He felt too little to help
questions.explanation: Max felt sad because he thought he was too little to be a good helper like his big sister Wren.

questions.question: What was Wren's special idea?
choices: Let Max crawl under the workbench
questions.explanation: Wren realized that Max's small size was actually perfect for crawling under the workbench to find lost items.

questions.question: What did Max find under the workbench?
questions.explanation: Max found seven balls, three toy cars, and Mom's missing earring under the workbench.

questions.question: How did Wren help Max feel better?
choices: She found a way Max could help too
She told him to go play
questions.explanation: Wren helped by thinking of a special job that was perfect for Max's size and abilities.

questions.question: What did the family learn on Helper Day?
choices: Everyone can help in their own way
questions.explanation: The family learned that everyone has special abilities and can contribute, no matter how small they are.

pages.title: Helper Day Words
pages.instructions: Fill in the missing words from the story!
pages.text: On Helper Day, Wren and Max wanted to _____ their family. Max felt _____ because he was small. But Wren had a great _____! Max could crawl under the workbench because he was the perfect _____. Everyone was _____ when Max found the lost toys!

pages.title: Family Feelings
pages.instructions: Choose the word that best describes how each character felt!
pages.text: At first, Wren felt _____ about getting an important job. Max felt _____ when he couldn't help with big boxes. When Dad saw Wren's kind idea, he felt _____ of her. At the end, Max felt _____ because he was a special helper too!
